---
name: Good First Issue
about: Create a small, clearly-scoped starter issue for contributors.
title: "[good first issue] "
labels: ["good first issue"]
assignees: []
---

## Summary

Describe the specific problem or missing improvement in one short paragraph.

## Why This Is Starter-Friendly

- narrow scope
- limited number of files
- clear verify path

## Start Here

- [ ] List the first file or directory to open
- [ ] List the next file if the issue spans more than one place

## Definition Of Done

- [ ] The intended behavior or docs improvement is clear
- [ ] Tests or docs updates are included when needed
- [ ] The issue stays inside the original scope

## Verify

Add the exact command, test file, or manual flow a contributor should use before opening a pull request.

## Out Of Scope

List the tempting follow-up work that should not be bundled into this issue.

## Difficulty

- [ ] Beginner
- [ ] Intermediate
- [ ] Advanced small scope

## Notes For Maintainers

- Keep `good first issue` only if the task is still first-time-friendly.
- If the scope grows, remove `good first issue` and keep `help wanted`.
- Point contributors at `docs/good-first-issues.md` when you want to show similar starter tasks.
